title: Dancers and choreographers
description: Teach or instruct individuals or groups for the primary purpose of self-enrichment or recreation, rather than for an occupational objective, educational attainment, competition, or fitness. Teach students on a short-term basis as a temporary replacement for a regular classroom teacher, typically using the regular teacher's lesson plan. Instruct individual students or small groups of students in academic subjects to support formal class instruction or to prepare students for standardized or admissions tests. (Based on 14 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Monitor the field of dance to remain aware of current trends and innovations.
Perform classical, modern, or acrobatic dances in productions, expressing stories, rhythm, and sound with bodies.
Advise dancers on standing and moving properly, teaching correct techniques to prevent injuries.
Choose music, sound effects, or spoken narratives to accompany a dance.
Design dances for a variety of settings and dancers, from beginners to professionals.
Read and study storylines and musical scores to translate ideas and moods into dance movements.
Direct rehearsals to instruct dancers in techniques and achieve desired effects.
Harmonize body movements with the rhythm of musical accompaniment.
Collaborate with choreographers to refine or modify dance steps.
Audition performers for dance parts and coordinate dancing with partners or ensembles.
Attend costume fittings and makeup calls associated with performances.
Audition for dance roles or for membership in dance companies.
Develop self-understanding of physical capabilities and limitations to choose appropriate dance styles.
Teach dance students and provide instruction on rhythm and interpretive movement.
Devise and choreograph dances for self or others.
Coordinate production music with music directors and develop ideas for creating dances.
Direct and stage dance presentations for various forms of entertainment, and document movements and technical aspects of choreography.
Seek influences from other art forms and experiment with different types of dances and feedback from dancers.
Assess students' dancing abilities to determine improvements needed.
Train, exercise, and attend dance classes to maintain high levels of technical proficiency and physical fitness.
Study and practice dance moves required for roles and perform in productions as needed.
Manage or assist in the management of dance schools.
Restage traditional dances in dance companies' repertoires and develop new interpretations.
Supervise student activities and provide feedback to encourage development.
Conduct individual visits and outings to engage students.
Organize and supervise recreational activities, ensuring participation and interaction.
Prepare lesson plans or learning modules according to the needs and goals of students.
Evaluate student progress and maintain accurate and complete records of their performance.
Attend professional meetings and workshops to improve tutoring practices and professional competence.
Confer with other teachers and professionals to plan and schedule lessons promoting learning and development.

Plan and conduct activities for a balanced program of instruction, demonstration, and work time that provides students with opportunities to observe, question, and investigate. Prepare materials and classrooms for class activities and establish clear objectives for all lessons, units, and projects and communicate those objectives to students.

Instruct students individually and in groups, using various teaching methods, such as lectures, discussions, and demonstrations, and monitor students' performance to suggest improvements, ensuring they satisfy course standards and objectives. Adapt teaching methods and instructional materials to meet students' varying needs and interests.

Encourage students to explore learning opportunities and to persevere with challenging tasks to prepare them for further development. Conduct classes, workshops, and demonstrations, providing individual instruction to teach topics and skills such as dancing.
Observe students to determine their qualifications, limitations, abilities, interests, and other individual characteristics.

Attend staff meetings and serve on committees, as required. Schedule tutoring appointments with students or their parents and prepare and facilitate tutoring workshops, collaborative projects, or academic support sessions for small groups of students.
Research or recommend textbooks, software, equipment, or other learning materials to complement tutoring. Write instructional articles on designated subjects.
Organize and supervise games and other recreational activities to promote physical, mental, and social development.
